scientific_integrity.detail: Hard verification rule: never output a paper unless real and verifiable

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 literature collection planning only

reliability.note: Strong verification rules; when live search unavailable, skill plans search strategy but cannot retrieve actual papers — this boundary needs explicit labeling

agent_specific.note: Four-tier priority layering and deduplication readiness are strong composability features; excellent ecosystem fit as upstream skill

inputs.label: Cross-database collection for gastric precancerous lesion intervention research

assertions.text: Database selection explained with justification per database
Search strategy with synonyms, controlled vocabulary, and database-adapted syntax produced
Preprints labeled separately from peer-reviewed papers
Priority tiers (Tier 1/2/3/P) assigned to candidate records
DOI explicitly noted as unavailable rather than invented when unverifiable
Source-database metadata preserved per record
Deduplication and screening readiness section present
Blind spots and coverage limitations explicitly noted
Next-step downstream routing recommendation given
No fabricated literature items

inputs.label: Lupus single-cell studies last 5 years including preprints, labeled separately

assertions.text: Time window filter applied to all databases
Preprints labeled as Tier P with explicit non-peer-reviewed status
Record schema includes direct link, DOI, evidence status, tier
No preprints conflated with peer-reviewed papers
Broad recall prioritized over narrow early filtering

inputs.label: Very broad topic requiring scope narrowing before collection

assertions.text: Topic too broad identified and narrowed before collection begins
Assumptions for scope narrowing explicitly stated
Question clarification recommended before formal collection
Collection not launched with unmanageably broad query
Practical collection target defined with boundary rationale

inputs.label: Offline execution — skill cannot retrieve actual papers

assertions.text: Skill correctly produces search strategy and database plan even without live retrieval
Candidate paper listing attempted only for verifiable papers from training knowledge
Unverifiable items labeled 'could not be confirmed' rather than invented
User informed that actual collection requires live database access
Search strategy output clearly labeled as a plan requiring user execution
assertions.note: The search plan produced was partially framed as if collection had been completed rather than as a user-execution plan

key_strengths: Hard verification rule preventing any non-real literature from appearing in the output is the strongest integrity safeguard for a literature collection skill
Database-specific adaptation (different syntax per database) reflects sophisticated search-strategy engineering
Four-tier priority layering with separate preprint tier provides excellent screening-ready organization
Blind spots and coverage limitations section prevents false completeness claims

recommendations.title: Offline execution mode not clearly framed as plan-only rather than completed collection
recommendations.problem: When live retrieval is unavailable, the search strategy output is sometimes framed as if collection has been completed rather than explicitly stating this is a user-execution plan.
recommendations.root_cause: No explicit offline-mode framing instruction in the skill.
recommendations.fix: Add an offline-mode instruction: 'When live database access is unavailable, all outputs in Sections C-H must be explicitly labeled as [SEARCH PLAN — requires user execution in the named databases]. Do not present candidate records as retrieved unless they can be verified from training knowledge with full metadata.'

recommendations.title: No guidance on handling duplicate DOIs from different databases
recommendations.problem: The deduplication readiness section notes duplicate-prone fields but does not specify how to handle papers that have the same DOI but different metadata across databases.
recommendations.root_cause: dedup-and-screening-readiness.md likely handles this, but SKILL.md does not surface a quick disambiguation rule.
recommendations.fix: Add a deduplication note: 'When the same DOI appears across multiple databases with conflicting metadata (e.g., different years or title formatting), use PubMed as the authoritative source for biomedical papers and preserve all source-database attributions.'
